name: Alto de la Burrica Loop from María
name_original: Desde María a la cima de la sierra — paseo circular
description: This difficult 12.7-mile circular hike from María to the top of the sierra offers 2805 feet of elevation gain and panoramic views.
region_intro: Hiking around María offers access to the Sierra María-Los Vélez Natural Park, characterized by mountainous terrain and diverse ecosystems. The region features pine forests, high-altitude plateaus, and rocky outcrops, providing varied landscapes for outdoor activities. Elevation changes are common, with trails ascending to viewpoints and through valleys. The area is suitable for hikers seeking natural environments with distinct geological formations. Best hiking trails around María The most popular hiking route is Alto de la Burrica Loop from María, a 12.7 miles (20.4 km) trail that takes 6 hours 28 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Collado de Portalchico loop from Los Vélez, a moderate 9.5 miles (15.3 km) path. This trail navigates through varied landscapes, including ascents to a mountain pass. Local hikers also love the Puntal del Morral Viewpoint loop from SL-A 108 Pinar de la Alfahuara, a 5.3 miles (8.6 km) trail leading through pine forest, often completed in about 2 hours 29 minutes. Hiking around María is defined by mountainous paths, pine forests, and elevated viewpoints. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in María are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 240 hikers have used komoot to explore María's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hiking trails around María?
A: Hiking around María primarily features mountainous terrain within the Sierra María-Los Vélez Natural Park. You'll encounter diverse ecosystems, including pine forests, high-altitude plateaus, and rocky outcrops. Elevation changes are common, with trails ascending to various viewpoints and traversing valleys.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in María?
A: Yes, María offers a good selection of trails suitable for beginners. Out of the 36 available routes, 11 are classified as easy. These trails provide a gentler introduction to the region's natural beauty without significant elevation challenges.

Q: What are some challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in María?
A: For experienced trekkers seeking a challenge, María has several difficult routes. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1224396338/alto-de-la-burrica-loop-from-maria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Burrica Loop from María&lt;/a&gt;, a 12.7-mile (20.4 km) trail with significant elevation gain, typically taking over 6 hours to complete. Another challenging route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38139137" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de Portalchico loop from María&lt;/a&gt;, covering 16.9 km with substantial ascents.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in María?
A: Yes, many of the trails around María are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21847264" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puntal del Morral Viewpoint loop from SL-A 108 Pinar de la Alfahuara&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 5.3-mile (8.6 km) circular trail through pine forests, offering scenic views.

Q: What landmarks or viewpoints can I discover on hikes in María?
A: Hiking trails in María lead to several interesting landmarks and viewpoints. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2784897" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caños de Caravaca Fountain&lt;/a&gt;, enjoy panoramic vistas from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2074254" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Sierra de María&lt;/a&gt;, or reach the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5292586" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puntal del Morral Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6134128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of the Inscriptions&lt;/a&gt; also offers a unique point of interest.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in María?
A: The hiking trails in María are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 50 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, including mountainous paths, serene pine forests, and the rewarding elevated viewpoints. The variety of routes catering to different ability levels is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in María?
A: While the region is known for its mountainous terrain, there are family-friendly options among the 11 easy routes available. These trails are generally shorter and have less elevation gain, making them suitable for families with children. Always check the specific route details for distance and elevation to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: Is María a dog-friendly hiking destination?
A: The Sierra María-Los Vélez Natural Park is generally welcoming to dogs, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock. Check individual trail regulations for any specific restrictions, and ensure you carry enough water for both yourself and your pet.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails around María?
A: Specific parking information varies by trailhead. Many routes, especially those starting from the town of María or other access points within the Sierra María-Los Vélez Natural Park, will have designated parking areas. It's advisable to check the starting point details of your chosen route on komoot for precise parking recommendations.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in María?
A: The best time to hike in María, located in the Sierra María-Los Vélez Natural Park, is typically during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant for outdoor activities, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential snow in winter at higher elevations. Spring brings blooming wildflowers, while autumn offers beautiful fall colors.

Q: Are there any cafes or pubs near the hiking trails in María?
A: The town of María itself, and other nearby villages within the Sierra María-Los Vélez Natural Park, offer local amenities including cafes and restaurants where you can refuel after your hike. While trails might not have facilities directly on them, you'll find options in the surrounding communities.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the 'From María to the top of the sierra — circular walk'?
faqs.answer: This circular walk is rated as difficult. It involves a significant elevation gain of approximately 855 meters over its 20.4 km distance, requiring good fitness and sure-footedness, especially on steeper sections.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular walk?
faqs.answer: The 'From María to the top of the sierra — circular walk' is estimated to take around 6 hours and 30 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain varies, starting with generally accessible paths. As you gain elevation, you'll encounter sections that transition from dirt trails to paths with stones, particularly on steeper climbs. Sure-footedness is often required.

faqs.question: Are there any specific landmarks or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, a significant landmark on this route is the La Burrica Summit (2,041 m) – María Geodetic Point. From higher points, you can expect wide-reaching views of the surrounding mountain ranges and the natural park.

faqs.question: Where does the 'From María to the top of the sierra — circular walk' start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for this exact trailhead are not provided, trails in the Sierra de María-Los Vélez Natural Park generally have accessible starting points. We recommend checking local maps or the komoot app for the most precise trailhead location and nearby parking options in María, Almería.

faqs.question: Is the 'From María to the top of the sierra — circular walk' dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de María-Los Vélez Natural Park generally allows dogs, but they should be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Always check for specific local regulations or signage at the trailhead.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this route is typically during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. The presence of shaded pine forests can offer some relief on hotter days, but summer can still be very warm. Winter might bring colder temperatures and potentially snow at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike in the Parque Natural de Sierra de María-Los Vélez?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking on public trails within the Parque Natural de Sierra de María-Los Vélez. However, it's always advisable to check the official park website for any updated regulations or special requirements before your visit.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular walk also passes through sections of the official trail known as La Umbría de la Virgen.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this difficult circular hike?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and duration, you should pack plenty of water, snacks, appropriate hiking footwear with good ankle support, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), layers of clothing, and a first-aid kit. A map or GPS device is also recommended.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or pubs near the starting point in María, Almería?
faqs.answer: María, Almería, as a local town, likely offers cafes and pubs where you can refuel before or after your hike. It's a good idea to check local listings or ask residents for recommendations upon arrival.
